title: Bias and Fairness in Large Language Models: A Survey
contribution: Provides three taxonomies—for bias evaluation metrics (embedding/probability/generated-text level), evaluation datasets, and mitigation techniques—synthesizing the evaluation landscape for LLM fairness and identifying open challenges.
taxonomy_cells: foundations / bias evaluation and mitigation survey

title: Towards Implicit Bias Detection and Mitigation in Multi-Agent LLM Interactions
contribution: Finds that LLMs generate gender-biased outputs in over 50% of multi-agent interactions, and that biases intensify through agent conversations; proposes self-reflection with in-context examples and supervised fine-tuning as effective mitigation strategies.
taxonomy_cells: implicit bias in multi-agent interaction / mitigation

title: FairMT-Bench: Benchmarking Fairness for Multi-turn Dialogue in Conversational LLMs
contribution: First fairness benchmark for multi-turn dialogue, with FairMT-10K dataset covering context understanding, user interaction, and instruction trade-off tasks, finding LLMs are significantly more biased in multi-turn settings than single-turn baselines.
taxonomy_cells: Multi-turn fairness evaluation / bias accumulation across interaction turns

title: Bias Runs Deep: Implicit Reasoning Biases in Persona-Assigned LLMs
contribution: Shows that assigning demographic personas to LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT-3.5) triggers implicit stereotyping across 24 reasoning datasets: 80% of personas degrade performance, with drops exceeding 70% on some datasets, even when models openly reject stereotypes when asked directly.

title: Emergent Bias and Fairness in Multi-Agent Decision Systems
contribution: Through large-scale simulations of multi-agent LLM systems on financial decision-making tasks (10 frontier models, 5 providers), shows that bias can emerge at the system level from agent interactions and communication patterns even when individual components appear fair, requiring holistic evaluation.
taxonomy_cells: emergent bias in multi-agent collaboration / system-level fairness evaluation

title: BiasBusters: Uncovering and Mitigating Tool Selection Bias in Large Language Models
contribution: Benchmarks tool-selection bias in seven LLMs across functionally equivalent tool providers, finding models fixate on single providers or favor earlier-listed tools, and proposes a filter-then-sample mitigation strategy.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — agent tool choice

title: From Biased Chatbots to Biased Agents: Examining Role Assignment Effects on LLM Agent Robustness
contribution: Demonstrates that demographic persona assignments to autonomous LLM agents cause up to 26.2% performance degradation across strategic reasoning, planning, and technical benchmarks, driven solely by task-irrelevant identity cues.
taxonomy_cells: role assignment bias / agentic performance fairness
planning-decomposition-bias / planning quality disparities across demographic groups

title: Fairness in Large Language Models: A Taxonomic Survey
contribution: Systematically surveys algorithmic bias sources, fairness evaluation metrics, and mitigation algorithms in LLMs with coverage of toolkits and datasets, published in the target venue for this survey.

title: BOLD: Dataset and Metrics for Measuring Biases in Open-Ended Language Generation
venue: Proceedings of the 2021 ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT)
contribution: Provides 23,679 text generation prompts across five demographic domains plus automated bias metrics for open-ended LLM generation, filling a gap not addressed by classification-focused benchmarks.

title: Counterfactual Fairness
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
contribution: Foundational causal definition of counterfactual fairness: a decision is fair if identical across the actual world and a counterfactual world where the individual belonged to a different demographic group, grounding evaluation in Pearl's causal framework.

title: Counterfactual Fairness Evaluation of LLM-Based Contact Center Agent Quality Assurance System
contribution: Introduces CFR (Counterfactual Flip Rate) and MASD (Mean Absolute Score Difference) as metrics for agent QA fairness, evaluating 18 LLMs on 3,000 contact-center transcripts across 13 identity/context/behavioral dimensions.

title: StereoSet: Measuring Stereotypical Bias in Pretrained Language Models
contribution: Defines the iCAT score combining a Language Modeling Score and Stereotype Score to jointly assess fluency and stereotype preference in LLMs across gender, profession, race, and religion domains.

title: A Multi-LLM Debiasing Framework
contribution: Proposes centralized and decentralized multi-LLM dialogue strategies for bias reduction, showing that coordinated multi-agent interaction significantly outperforms single-model debiasing baselines across multiple protected social groups.
taxonomy_cells: multi-agent debiasing framework / bias mitigation in LLM collaboration

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
contribution: Seminal work introducing LLM-powered social simulation agents with persistent memory and persona, establishing the foundational architecture for role-based multi-agent societies and raising questions about emergent group-level biases in persona-driven agent systems.
taxonomy_cells: multi-agent social simulation / persona and role design for agents

title: BBQ: A Hand-Built Bias Benchmark for Question Answering
contribution: Introduces a 58,492-question benchmark testing stereotype reliance in ambiguous vs. disambiguated QA contexts across nine social dimensions, providing group-bias metrics as the difference in accuracy between stereotype-aligned and stereotype-conflicting correct answers.

title: A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents
contribution: Provides a comprehensive systematic review of LLM-based autonomous agents covering architecture (memory, planning, action), applications, and evaluation, serving as a primary agent-structure reference for this survey.

title: Bias Amplification in RAG: Poisoning Knowledge Retrieval to Steer LLMs
contribution: Proposes BRRA framework showing that adversarially poisoned retrieval corpora amplify demographic stereotypes in LLM outputs, establishing a direct link between retrieval security and model fairness.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — retrieval source bias propagated through agents
memory-retrieval-bias / retrieval bias amplification / adversarial memory

title: LLMs are Biased Teachers: Evaluating LLM Bias in Personalized Education
contribution: Evaluates nine LLMs across 17,000+ educational explanations and finds that models generate demographically biased content differentiated by race, gender, income, and disability status, with income-related bias highest.

title: Does RAG Introduce Unfairness in LLMs? Evaluating Fairness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
contribution: Proposes a scenario-based fairness evaluation framework for RAG pipelines and empirically shows that fairness issues persist in both the retrieval and generation stages across gender and geographic demographics.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — retrieval source selection fairness (adjacent: RAG)

title: The Rise and Potential of Large Language Model Based Agents: A Survey
contribution: Surveys LLM-based agents in single-agent, multi-agent, and human-agent settings, characterising perception, memory, and action components and discussing safety concerns, anchoring the agent-architecture background of this survey.

title: DUCX: Decomposing Unfairness in Tool-Using Chest X-ray Agents
contribution: Identifies three agent-specific fairness failure modes in tool-using medical agents — tool exposure bias, tool transition (routing) bias, and model reasoning bias — with demographic subgroup gaps exceeding 50% conditioned on tool availability.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — demographic disparities in tool routing
planning-decomposition-bias / planning quality disparities across demographic groups

title: mFARM: Towards Multi-Faceted Fairness Assessment based on HARMs in Clinical Decision Support
contribution: Proposes a multi-metric mFARM score combining Allocational, Stability, and Latent disparity sub-metrics into a single clinical-readiness score, paired with two MIMIC-IV-derived benchmarks of over 50,000 demographically varied prompts.

title: BiasDPO: Mitigating Bias in Language Models through Direct Preference Optimization
contribution: Applies DPO with a curated dataset of biased/unbiased response pairs to train Phi-2, achieving substantial reductions in biased outputs on standard bias benchmarks without standard RLHF infrastructure.

title: Equal Access, Unequal Interaction: A Counterfactual Audit of LLM Fairness
contribution: Conducts a controlled counterfactual audit of GPT-4 and LLaMA-3.1-70B over career-advice tasks, showing zero refusal disparity but significant interaction-quality disparities (sentiment, politeness, hedging) across age, gender, and nationality.

title: Measuring gender and racial biases in large language models: Intersectional evidence from automated resume evaluation
contribution: Scores ~361,000 randomized resumes across five LLMs and finds pro-female but anti-Black-male bias that would shift hiring probabilities by 1-3 percentage points.

title: Understanding Intrinsic Socioeconomic Biases in Large Language Models
contribution: Creates a 1M-sentence dataset showing LLMs (GPT-2, Llama 2, Falcon) extract demographic attributes from names and correlate them with socioeconomic stereotypes, amplified by intersectionality.

title: Intrinsic Meets Extrinsic Fairness: Assessing the Downstream Impact of Bias Mitigation in Large Language Models
contribution: Provides a unified evaluation framework comparing intrinsic (concept unlearning) vs. extrinsic (counterfactual data augmentation) bias mitigation, measuring Demographic Parity, Accuracy Parity, and Equality of Odds on financial classification tasks.

title: Accept or Deny? Evaluating LLM Fairness and Performance in Loan Approval across Table-to-Text Serialization Approaches
contribution: Benchmarks LLM fairness in loan-approval decisions across three countries and multiple serialization formats, showing certain formats improve accuracy while exacerbating fairness disparities, highlighting the need for context-specific fairness metrics.

title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
contribution: Proposes Constitutional AI, training LLMs to critique and revise their own outputs guided by a written set of principles (RLHF-from-AI-feedback), a process-level constitutional intervention for reducing harmful and biased outputs.

title: When Names Change Verdicts: Intervention Consistency Reveals Systematic Bias in LLM Decision-Making
contribution: Introduces ICE-Guard, a framework measuring demographic, authority, and framing flip rates in LLM decisions, finding authority/framing biases exceed demographic bias and that structured decomposition reduces flip rates by up to 100%.

title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
venue: Proceedings of the 2021 ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT)
contribution: Identifies environmental, financial, and social harms of large LMs including bias amplification from uncurated training data, influencing the framing of responsible LLM development and fairness discourse.
taxonomy_cells: foundations / LLM risks and societal harms

title: How Can We Diagnose and Treat Bias in Large Language Models for Clinical Decision-Making?
contribution: Introduces the Counterfactual Patient Variations (CPV) dataset from JAMA Clinical Challenge and shows that debiasing one demographic dimension (gender) can introduce bias along another (ethnicity), revealing multi-dimensional bias interactions.

title: On the Opportunities and Risks of Foundation Models
contribution: Coins the term 'foundation models' and comprehensively analyses their capabilities and risks including fairness, inequity, and misuse, establishing the paradigm within which LLM fairness research operates.

title: Bring Your Own Prompts: Use-Case-Specific Bias and Fairness Evaluation for LLMs (LangFair)
contribution: Maps deployment contexts to appropriate fairness metrics (toxicity, stereotyping, counterfactual unfairness, allocational harm) via a decision framework and releases the LangFair open-source Python library for practical use-case-grounded evaluation.

title: Measuring and Mitigating Racial Disparities in LLMs: Evidence from a Mortgage Underwriting Experiment
contribution: Audit study using real loan applications and manipulated race signals finds LLMs deny more loans and charge higher interest rates to Black applicants; simple anti-bias prompts substantially reduce the disparity.

title: I Want to Break Free! Persuasion and Anti-Social Behavior of LLMs in Multi-Agent Settings with Social Hierarchy
contribution: Examines 2,400 LLM conversations in a Stanford Prison Experiment-inspired hierarchy (guard/prisoner roles), finding that assigned social roles significantly shape persuasion success and anti-social behavior, and that harmful conduct emerges even without explicit negative persona prompts.
taxonomy_cells: social-role hierarchy bias / role-play harms in multi-agent settings

title: MaxMin-RLHF: Alignment with Diverse Human Preferences
contribution: Introduces a MaxMin RLHF objective inspired by social-choice egalitarianism that learns a mixture of reward functions, improving minority-group win-rates by 33% over standard RLHF while preserving majority performance.

title: When Identity Skews Debate: Anonymization for Bias-Reduced Multi-Agent Reasoning
contribution: Shows that LLM agents in multi-agent debate exhibit identity-driven sycophancy (following peer opinions over content merit) and self-bias; proposes response anonymization and the Identity Bias Coefficient metric to achieve content-based rather than identity-based reasoning.
taxonomy_cells: identity-driven sycophancy in multi-agent debate / bias mitigation

title: Unmasking Conversational Bias in AI Multiagent Systems
contribution: Creates controlled echo-chamber experiments with paired LLMs and finds significant stance shifts aligned with liberal model bias that current questionnaire-based bias detectors fail to identify.
taxonomy_cells: memory-retrieval-bias / conversational bias accumulation in multi-agent systems

title: Bias and Unfairness in Information Retrieval Systems: New Challenges in the LLM Era
venue: Proceedings of the 30th ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining
contribution: Surveys how LLM integration into IR systems (search engines, recommenders) creates novel bias and unfairness challenges across data collection, model development, and evaluation, framing them as distribution mismatch problems.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — adjacent: LLM-era IR bias survey covering retrieval tools agents rely on

title: CFaiRLLM: Consumer Fairness Evaluation in Large-Language Model Recommender System
contribution: Proposes an evaluation framework distinguishing genuine personalization from unfair demographic bias in LLM recommenders, incorporating intersectional fairness across overlapping sensitive attributes.

title: A Normative Framework for Benchmarking Consumer Fairness in Large Language Model Recommender Systems
contribution: Proposes a principled evaluation framework revealing statistically significant age-based fairness deviations in LLM recommenders and arguing conventional collaborative-filtering metrics are insufficient for LLM-based systems.

title: Building Guardrails for Large Language Models
contribution: Surveys existing open-source safeguarding tools and proposes a systematic guardrail construction approach combining neural-symbolic methods and input/output filtering to prevent harmful, biased content in LLM deployments.

title: Causal-Guided Active Learning for Debiasing Large Language Models
contribution: Combines active learning with causal mechanisms to autonomously identify biased samples and induce bias patterns, then uses in-context learning to prevent models from relying on identified biases.

title: Fairness Through Awareness
venue: Proceedings of the 3rd Innovations in Theoretical Computer Science Conference (ITCS)
contribution: Introduces the metric-based individual fairness definition requiring that similar individuals be treated similarly, establishing a foundational fairness concept adopted throughout algorithmic fairness literature.

title: Empowering Many, Biasing a Few: Generalist Credit Scoring through Large Language Models
contribution: Proposes CALM, the first LLM-based credit scoring framework with a 9-dataset benchmark, empirically documenting demographic bias risks in LLM-driven credit decisions alongside performance gains over classical models.

title: Thinking Fair and Slow: On the Efficacy of Structured Prompts for Debiasing Language Models
contribution: Systematically evaluates System-2-inspired structured prompting strategies (implicative, role-based, multi-step) for debiasing LLMs without retraining, showing implicative prompts achieve the lowest mean bias on downstream tasks.

title: Accumulating Context Changes the Beliefs of Language Models
contribution: Quantifies how accumulated conversational context causes substantial belief drift in LLMs (up to 54.7% shift after 10 rounds) and shows these belief changes translate into behavioral changes in agentic tool-use tasks.
taxonomy_cells: memory-retrieval-bias / context-window history effects on fairness

title: MEDEQUALQA: Evaluating Biases in LLMs with Counterfactual Reasoning
contribution: Builds a 69,000-item QA-level counterfactual benchmark that perturbs only patient pronouns across clinical vignettes, isolating reasoning-level gender bias in medical LLMs while holding symptoms constant.

title: Guiding LLM Decision-Making with Fairness Reward Models
contribution: Shows naive chain-of-thought sampling amplifies demographic bias in high-stakes decisions; introduces a transferable Fairness Reward Model that down-weights biased reasoning trajectories to improve equitable outcomes.
taxonomy_cells: planning-decomposition-bias / decision-making bias in agentic planning

title: Equality of Opportunity in Supervised Learning
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
contribution: Proposes the equalized odds and equal opportunity group fairness criteria and shows how to post-process any classifier to satisfy them, a canonical group fairness definition referenced in LLM fairness work.

title: Measuring Sycophancy of Language Models in Multi-turn Dialogues
contribution: Introduces SYCON Bench to measure stance-flip dynamics across multi-turn conversations in 17 models, finding that alignment tuning amplifies sycophantic bias drift while model scaling and reasoning optimization resist it.
taxonomy_cells: Multi-turn fairness drift / sycophancy and bias accumulation over dialogue turns

title: Achieving Long-Term Fairness in Sequential Decision Making
contribution: Introduces a causal path-specific effects framework on time-lagged causal graphs to measure long-term fairness in sequential decisions, recasting the problem as constrained performative risk minimization with convergence guarantees.
taxonomy_cells: Longitudinal fairness in sequential decisions / temporal causal drift

title: No Free Lunch: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Undermines Fairness in LLMs, Even for Vigilant Users
contribution: Introduces a three-tier threat framework showing that RAG can undermine fairness alignment without fine-tuning, even when users actively curate retrieved corpora.

title: LLMs on Trial: Evaluating Judicial Fairness for Large Language Models
contribution: Tests 16 LLMs on 177,100 judicial case scenarios across 65 labels and finds pervasive demographic bias, inconsistency, and an accuracy-fairness tradeoff in LLM judicial decision-making.

title: Collective Constitutional AI: Aligning a Language Model with Public Input
contribution: Fine-tunes an LLM using a publicly-sourced constitution derived from citizen input, achieving lower bias scores across nine social dimensions while maintaining benchmark performance.

title: Reasoning Towards Fairness: Mitigating Bias in Language Models through Reasoning-Guided Fine-Tuning
contribution: Establishes that stronger reasoning capability correlates with lower stereotypical bias; proposes ReGiFT to transfer reasoning patterns from advanced models to weaker ones, improving fairness without fairness-specific training data.

title: Stereotype or Personalization? User Identity Biases Chatbot Recommendations
contribution: Shows that chatbots produce racially stereotypical recommendations based on both explicit and implicit user identity cues, while concealing this fact from users when asked.

title: Multiple LLM Agents Debate for Equitable Cultural Alignment
contribution: Proposes a two-agent debate framework for cultural norm alignment tested across 75 countries and 21 model combinations; demonstrates that multi-agent debate improves both accuracy and cross-cultural fairness, enabling smaller models to match larger ones.
taxonomy_cells: multi-agent debate for cultural fairness / bias mitigation across groups

title: Towards Fair RAG: On the Impact of Fair Ranking in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
contribution: Across 12 RAG models and 7 tasks, demonstrates that fairness-aware retrieval ranking maintains or improves generation quality while producing more balanced source attribution.
taxonomy_cells: memory-retrieval-bias / RAG fairness / fair ranking mitigation

title: Mitigating Bias in RAG: Controlling the Embedder
contribution: Identifies a linear bias-conflict relationship between LLM, embedder, and corpus components in RAG, and shows that tuning embedder bias is more effective than naive fairness maximisation for achieving equitable RAG output.
taxonomy_cells: memory-retrieval-bias / RAG retrieval bias / component-level mitigation

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
contribution: Introduces RAG, combining parametric language model memory with a non-parametric dense retrieval index to produce more factual, specific, and diverse outputs on knowledge-intensive tasks.
taxonomy_cells: Agent architecture / memory and knowledge retrieval

title: Fairness of ChatGPT
contribution: Provides the first systematic cross-domain fairness evaluation of ChatGPT covering education, criminology, finance, and healthcare, measuring both group and individual fairness under biased and unbiased prompts.

title: Prompting Fairness: Integrating Causality to Debias Large Language Models
contribution: Proposes a causality-guided prompting framework that identifies how social information flows through causal pathways in LLMs and applies prompting strategies to regulate those pathways, unifying existing prompt-based debiasing methods.

title: Actions Speak Louder than Words: Agent Decisions Reveal Implicit Biases in Language Models
contribution: Proposes measuring implicit bias through LLM agent decision disparities under sociodemographically-informed personas, finding significant disparities across six LLMs with advanced models exhibiting greater implicit biases despite reduced explicit biases.
taxonomy_cells: planning-decomposition-bias / decision-making bias in agentic planning

title: FairSteer: Inference Time Debiasing for LLMs with Dynamic Activation Steering
contribution: Proposes an inference-time debiasing method exploiting linear separability of bias signatures in hidden activations (>90%), dynamically steering activations via contrastive debiasing vectors without model retraining or prompt customization.

title: From Single to Societal: Analyzing Persona-Induced Bias in Multi-Agent Interactions
contribution: Reveals that in multi-agent LLM systems, assigned personas create measurable in-group favoritism (conformity bias toward same-persona agents) and differential trust based on demographic privilege, persisting across different LLMs and interaction configurations.
taxonomy_cells: persona-induced bias / in-group favoritism in multi-agent collaboration

title: Assessing Dialect Fairness and Robustness of Large Language Models in Reasoning Tasks
contribution: Introduces ReDial, a benchmark of 1,200+ parallel Standard English / AAVE reasoning query pairs, showing significant brittleness and unfairness in GPT, Claude, Llama, Mistral, and Phi families.

title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
venue: Transactions of the Association for Computational Linguistics (TACL)
contribution: Establishes that LLM retrieval performance degrades for information in the middle of long contexts, creating systematic positional bias that differentially disadvantages documents by rank position.
taxonomy_cells: memory-retrieval-bias / retrieval position bias / context-window effects

title: Truth Decay: Quantifying Multi-Turn Sycophancy in Language Models
contribution: Quantifies how sycophantic bias compounds across multi-turn conversations, showing that accuracy systematically decreases and models increasingly align with user input rather than reassessing prior errors.
taxonomy_cells: Multi-turn fairness drift / sycophancy compounding across interaction turns

title: How Implicit Bias Accumulates and Propagates in LLM Long-term Memory
contribution: Shows that implicit bias in memory-augmented LLMs intensifies over long-term interactions and propagates across unrelated social domains; proposes Dynamic Memory Tagging (DMT) as mitigation.
taxonomy_cells: Long-horizon bias drift in LLM memory / multi-turn compounding

title: Detecting race and gender bias in visual representation of AI on web search engines
contribution: Audits six search engines across race and gender representation, finding white-male over-representation in AI imagery — illustrating search-engine representational bias that LLM agents inherit when using web search as a tool.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — adjacent: search-engine bias inherited by agents using web search tools

title: Feedback Loop and Bias Amplification in Recommender Systems
contribution: Provides foundational simulation-based evidence that recommender feedback loops iteratively amplify popularity bias, reduce content diversity, and disproportionately harm minority-group users across successive interaction cycles.
taxonomy_cells: Feedback-loop fairness / bias amplification over trajectory iterations (seminal)

title: A Survey on Bias and Fairness in Machine Learning
contribution: Foundational ML fairness survey cataloguing 23 types of bias, reviewing fairness definitions and mitigation strategies across domains, providing the pre-LLM baseline for this survey's related-work section.

title: Rejected Dialects: Biases Against African American Language in Reward Models
contribution: Reveals that RLHF reward models score African American Language texts ~4% lower accuracy than White Mainstream English equivalents and steer conversations toward WME, embedding dialect discrimination into alignment.

title: MALIBU Benchmark: Multi-Agent LLM Implicit Bias Uncovered
contribution: Introduces MALIBU, a benchmark measuring implicit bias in multi-agent LLM systems using a two-phase demographic persona evaluation; finds that standard bias mitigation methods can overcorrect by favoring marginalized personas rather than achieving neutrality.

title: Evaluation and Benchmarking of LLM Agents: A Survey
contribution: Proposes a two-dimensional evaluation framework for LLM agents covering assessment targets (behavior, capabilities, dependability, safety) and methodology, with emphasis on enterprise deployment concerns.

title: CrowS-Pairs: A Challenge Dataset for Measuring Social Biases in Masked Language Models
contribution: Presents 1,508 crowdsourced sentence pairs covering nine bias types to benchmark stereotypical bias in masked language models, finding all tested MLMs substantially favor stereotyping sentences.

title: "You Gotta be a Doctor, Lin": An Investigation of Name-Based Bias of Large Language Models in Employment Recommendations
contribution: Over 750,000 prompts with 320 demographically coded names reveal LLMs prefer White female candidates for hiring while recommending salaries up to 5% lower for women vs. men.

title: The Social Cost of Intelligence: Emergence, Propagation, and Amplification of Stereotypical Bias in Multi-Agent Systems
contribution: Establishes that inter-agent communication can trigger up to 70% new bias emergence, propagate bias to over 80% of agents, and amplify stereotypes by more than 3x; also reveals that multi-agent systems are highly vulnerable to bias injection attacks.
taxonomy_cells: bias propagation and amplification in multi-agent systems

title: Sociodemographic biases in medical decision making by large language models
contribution: Analyzes 1.7 million LLM outputs from 1,000 ED cases across 32 sociodemographic variations, showing Black, unhoused, and LGBTQIA+ patients are systematically over-triaged and low-income patients denied advanced testing.

title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
contribution: Introduces InstructGPT, showing RLHF fine-tuning reduces toxic and biased outputs of GPT-3 while improving instruction-following; foundational RLHF-for-fairness reference.
taxonomy_cells: mitigation / RLHF fine-tuning for alignment and debiasing

title: Toward Systematic Counterfactual Fairness Evaluation of Large Language Models: The CAFFE Framework
contribution: Proposes CAFFE, a structured intent-aware framework that formalizes LLM fairness test cases, auto-generates counterfactual test data, and uses semantic-similarity metrics to detect bias, improving fairness-bug detection by up to 60% over metamorphic approaches.

title: Once Upon a Team: Investigating Bias in LLM-Driven Software Team Composition and Task Allocation
contribution: Across 3,000 simulated team-composition decisions, demonstrates that LLM agents allocate technical and leadership roles unevenly by candidate nationality and gender pronouns, reflecting stereotypes in agent-driven task planning.
taxonomy_cells: planning-decomposition-bias / planning quality disparities across demographic groups

title: SCOPE: A Dataset of Stereotyped Prompts for Counterfactual Fairness Assessment of LLMs
contribution: Releases 241,280 counterfactual prompt pairs across 9 bias dimensions, 1,536 demographic groups, and 4 communicative intents to enable systematic intent-aware counterfactual fairness evaluation of LLMs.

title: Presumed Cultural Identity: How Names Shape LLM Responses
contribution: Shows that LLMs make strong cultural-stereotype assumptions from user names across multiple languages and cultures, triggering biased suggestions even in ostensibly neutral suggestion-seeking queries.

title: Large Language Models Sensitivity to The Order of Options in Multiple-Choice Questions
contribution: Quantifies LLM positional selection bias (13–75% performance gap under reordering), demonstrating that when agents choose tools from ordered lists, earlier-appearing options gain a systematic unfair advantage.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — positional/order bias in LLM selection affecting tool choice

title: A Toolbox for Surfacing Health Equity Harms and Biases in Large Language Models
contribution: Introduces EquityMedQA, a collection of seven QA-level adversarial datasets (4,619 examples) plus a multifactorial human-assessment framework for surfacing health-equity harms in LLM-generated medical answers.

title: Bias patterns in the application of LLMs for clinical decision support: A comprehensive study
contribution: Evaluates eight LLMs on standardized clinical vignettes via red-teaming, finding disparities across protected patient attributes and that Chain-of-Thought prompting reduces but does not eliminate clinical bias.

title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
contribution: Trains LMs to self-supervisedly learn when and how to call external APIs (calculator, search, calendar) with minimal demonstrations, enabling zero-shot tool use without sacrificing language modeling.

title: FairSense: Long-Term Fairness Analysis of ML-Enabled Systems
contribution: Proposes a Monte-Carlo simulation framework that detects long-term fairness violations caused by self-reinforcing feedback loops in deployed ML systems, validated on loan lending, opioid risk scoring, and predictive policing.
taxonomy_cells: Long-horizon fairness analysis / feedback-loop-induced fairness drift in deployed systems

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
contribution: Proposes Reflexion, a framework enabling agents to improve via verbal self-reflection stored in episodic memory, avoiding weight updates and achieving 91% on HumanEval.

title: Old Habits Die Hard: How Conversational History Geometrically Traps LLMs
contribution: Uses Markov chain modelling and geometric analysis of latent representations to show that prior biased interactions create geometric traps in LLM hidden space that persist into future turns.
taxonomy_cells: memory-retrieval-bias / context-window history effects / bias persistence

title: Bias-Aware Agent: Enhancing Fairness in AI-Driven Knowledge Retrieval
venue: Companion Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2025 (WWW '25)
contribution: Integrates bias detectors as tools within an agentic retrieval framework to surface and mitigate embedded biases in retrieved content, promoting equitable information access.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — bias-aware tool integration for fair retrieval

title: Bias Mitigation Agent: Optimizing Source Selection for Fair and Balanced Knowledge Retrieval
contribution: Presents a multi-agent system that optimizes knowledge source selection to minimize bias, achieving an 81.82% reduction in biased content versus baseline retrieval approaches.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — source selection optimization for fairness

title: "I'm sorry to hear that": Finding New Biases in Language Models with a Holistic Descriptor Dataset
contribution: Introduces HolisticBias, a dataset of ~600 demographic descriptors across 13 axes generating 450,000+ sentence prompts, enabling broad-coverage bias evaluation in generative LLMs.

title: ToolTweak: An Attack on Tool Selection in LLM-based Agents
contribution: Demonstrates that iterative manipulation of tool names and descriptions biases agents toward specific tools (baseline 20% → 81% selection rate), creating unfair provider advantage and distributional shifts in tool ecosystems.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — adversarial tool description manipulation

title: Towards Large Language Models that Benefit for All: Benchmarking Group Fairness in Reward Models
contribution: Benchmarks group fairness in LLM reward models, showing all evaluated reward models exhibit statistically significant group unfairness, and disentangles bias contributions from pre-training vs. RLHF fine-tuning stages.

title: Beyond Memorization: Violating Privacy Via Inference with Large Language Models
contribution: Demonstrates that LLMs can infer sensitive personal attributes (location, income, sex) from innocuous user text with up to 85% accuracy, and that anonymization and alignment fail as defenses.

title: Cognitive Architectures for Language Agents
contribution: Proposes CoALA, a systematic framework organizing language agents around modular memory types, a structured action space, and a generalized decision-making process, grounded in cognitive science.
taxonomy_cells: Agent architecture / taxonomy and cognitive foundations

title: Is Your LLM-as-a-Recommender Agent Trustable? LLMs' Recommendation is Easily Hacked by Biases (Preferences)
contribution: Introduces BiasRecBench covering paper review, e-commerce, and job recruitment, showing that even state-of-the-art LLM agents (GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5-Pro) are systematically biased toward contextual cues rather than quality when acting as recommender agents.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — LLM-as-recommender agent bias and trustworthiness

title: Different Demographic Cues Yield Inconsistent Conclusions About LLM Personalization and Bias
contribution: Analyzes 14.8M prompts showing that different demographic markers (names vs. other cues) for the same group trigger only partially overlapping response changes in LLMs, calling for multi-cue evaluations.

title: Algorithms of Oppression: How Search Engines Reinforce Racism
contribution: Seminal book demonstrating that commercial search engine result curation systematically disadvantages people of color, providing foundational evidence that agents relying on web search APIs inherit structural racial biases.
taxonomy_cells: Tool/API Selection Bias — adjacent: seminal work on search-engine racial bias propagated through agent tool use

title: Agentic AI in Healthcare & Medicine: A Seven-Dimensional Taxonomy for Empirical Evaluation of LLM-based Agents
contribution: Reviews 49 healthcare LLM-agent studies across a seven-dimensional taxonomy revealing critical implementation gaps in event-triggered activation, drift detection, and action-oriented tasks.

title: Are Emily and Greg Still More Employable than Lakisha and Jamal? Investigating Algorithmic Hiring Bias in the Era of ChatGPT
contribution: Replicates the Bertrand-Mullainathan audit on GPT-3.5, Bard, Claude, and Llama for resume-job matching and summarization, finding models are largely robust on race/gender but biased on pregnancy and political affiliation.

title: Exploring Safety-Utility Trade-Offs in Personalized Language Models
contribution: Systematically documents personalization bias across Llama, Mistral, GPT-3.5, and GPT-4o — user identity affects both safety (harmful-prompt compliance) and utility (knowledge, math, coding) performance.

title: CEB: Compositional Evaluation Benchmark for Fairness in Large Language Models
contribution: Introduces a compositional taxonomy organizing bias datasets along three dimensions (bias type, social group, task), unifying evaluation across inconsistent prior benchmarks and showing that bias levels vary significantly across dimensions.

title: Observations and Remedies for Large Language Model Bias in Self-Consuming Performative Loop
contribution: Introduces the Self-Consuming Performative Loop (SCPL) framework for LLMs and shows that iterative fine-tuning on self-generated data amplifies preference bias and degrades fairness for underserved groups; proposes reward-based rejection sampling as mitigation.
taxonomy_cells: Iterative LLM self-training bias drift / feedback-loop fairness degradation

title: Ethical and Social Risks of Harm from Language Models
contribution: Provides a structured taxonomy of 21 risks from LLMs across six areas including discrimination and exclusion, forming a widely-cited harm framework for LLM safety and fairness research.

title: Gender, Race, and Intersectional Bias in Resume Screening via Language Model Retrieval
contribution: Audits Massive Text Embedding models on 500+ resumes across nine occupations, finding White-associated names favoured in 85% of cases and Black males disadvantaged in up to 100% of cases.

title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
contribution: Presents an open-source framework for building multi-agent systems where customizable, conversable agents collaborate via natural language and code across diverse tasks.

title: FMBench: Benchmarking Fairness in Multimodal Large Language Models on Medical Tasks
contribution: First benchmark for multimodal LLM fairness on medical VQA and report generation, covering 40,000 annotated instances across race, ethnicity, language, and gender, with a novel Fairness-Aware Performance (FAP) metric.

title: Does Reasoning Introduce Bias? A Study of Social Bias Evaluation and Mitigation in LLM Reasoning
contribution: Demonstrates that chain-of-thought reasoning steps in models like DeepSeek-R1 and o1 correlate with stereotypical outputs; proposes ADBP to detect bias by monitoring prediction shifts across reasoning stages.

title: Fairness Feedback Loops: Training on Synthetic Data Amplifies Bias
contribution: Formalizes model-induced distribution shift (MIDS) where model outputs pollute future training sets, degrading fairness and minority representation through successive retraining iterations; introduces algorithmic reparation as a remedy.
taxonomy_cells: Feedback-loop fairness degradation / iterative training bias amplification

title: On the Algorithmic Bias of Aligning Large Language Models with RLHF: Preference Collapse and Matching Regularization
venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association 2025
contribution: Identifies that KL-regularized RLHF causes preference collapse for minority groups and proposes Preference Matching RLHF with a novel regularizer that achieves 29-41% improvement in alignment with diverse human preferences.

title: FairMedQA: Benchmarking Bias in Large Language Models for Medical Question Answering
contribution: Introduces a QA-level medical fairness benchmark of 4,806 counterfactual USMLE vignette pairs varying race, sex, and SES, finding accuracy disparities of 3–19 pp across 12 LLMs and exposing biases ≥12 pp larger than prior CPV benchmark.

title: Adapting Static Fairness to Sequential Decision-Making: Bias Mitigation Strategies towards Equal Long-term Benefit Rate
contribution: Demonstrates that applying static fairness criteria in sequential MDP settings paradoxically increases temporal bias; introduces ELBERT fairness constraint and policy-gradient method to mitigate long-horizon discriminatory effects.
taxonomy_cells: Sequential decision fairness / temporal drift from static-to-dynamic fairness mismatch

title: Mitigating Social Bias in Large Language Models: A Multi-Objective Approach within a Multi-Agent Framework
contribution: Proposes MOMA, a multi-agent framework using causal interventions on bias-related inputs, reducing bias scores by up to 87.7% on BBQ while maintaining task accuracy with only marginal degradation.

title: Quantifying Fairness in LLMs Beyond Tokens: A Semantic and Statistical Perspective
contribution: Introduces FiSCo, a claim-level statistical framework formalizing group counterfactual fairness that applies entailment-based hypothesis testing to detect subtle semantic disparities in long-form LLM outputs across demographic groups.

title: Does the Prompt-based Large Language Model Recognize Students' Demographics and Introduce Bias in Essay Scoring?
contribution: Shows GPT-4o infers students' first-language background from 25,000+ essays and produces larger scoring errors for non-native English writers when demographic identity is correctly inferred.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
contribution: Introduces the ReAct prompting paradigm that interleaves reasoning traces with task-specific actions, enabling agents to query external knowledge sources and handle multi-step decision-making.

title: A Survey on Trustworthy LLM Agents: Threats and Countermeasures
contribution: Proposes TrustAgent framework examining threats and defenses for LLM agents across intrinsic dimensions (brain, memory, tool) and extrinsic aspects (user, agent, environment).

title: Coding Inequity: Assessing GPT-4's Potential for Perpetuating Racial and Gender Biases in Healthcare
contribution: Demonstrates that GPT-4 produces systematically biased diagnostic and treatment recommendations across race and gender in four clinical use cases including medical education and plan generation.

title: Causal Prompting: Debiasing Large Language Model Prompting based on Front-Door Adjustment
contribution: First work applying front-door causal adjustment to LLM prompting for debiasing, using chain-of-thought as a mediating variable to mitigate bias across seven NLP datasets without accessing model parameters.

title: Datasets for Fairness in Language Models: An In-Depth Survey
contribution: Surveys and characterizes 16 popular fairness datasets across provenance, demographic scope, annotation design, and intended use, proposing a unified evaluation framework that uncovers overlooked demographic biases distorting benchmark conclusions.

title: Evaluating the Effect of Retrieval Augmentation on Social Biases
contribution: Demonstrates across English, Japanese, and Chinese that biases in retrieved document collections are systematically amplified in RAG-generated responses even when the base LLM exhibits low baseline bias.

title: Veracity Bias and Beyond: Uncovering LLMs' Hidden Beliefs in Problem-Solving Reasoning
contribution: Identifies attribution and evaluation biases in LLM problem-solving reasoning, finding models disproportionately assign correct math/coding solutions to non-African-American groups; biases are embedded in reasoning processes not just surface outputs.
